notes: Opening opportunity openings:plaintiff. Objective: plaintiff opening on whether by May 24, 2026 11:59 PM ET Iran initiated a major non-weather airspace closure under the market rule. Record inspected via get_case/list_evidence/read_evidence_range. Packet contents: market-page rule capture is the only substantive admitted record; primary-evidence, official-source-record, secondary-reporting, and unresolved-record-gaps all say no merits packet has yet been built and identify needed sources. Operative elements: Iran initiation; non-weather; deadline; major closure either broad commercial flight closure/suspension in Iranian airspace or major region, or complete non-weather suspension of commercial arrivals/departures affecting at least two listed airports (IKA, THR, MHD, SYZ, IFN); primary resolution sources are official Iranian aviation authorities plus consensus credible reporting. Evidence analysis: current packet proves the rule and unresolved gaps; it does not prove an actual May 24 closure, NOTAM, official order, affected airports, exact timing, or Iran initiation. Provenance/custody of packet is system case_packet, juror-visible, text/plain with hashes; weight is high for market rule, low for merits occurrence because it expressly lacks merits evidence. Investigation/source map: primary targets are Iranian Civil Aviation Organization/CAA/Iran Airports and Air Navigation Company records, NOTAM text for OIIX/western Iran, airport operations records for IKA/THR/MHD/SYZ/IFN; confirming sources include Reuters/AP/Mehr/IRNA/Roya/CNN-News18/aviation risk services/flight tracking; adverse targets include official or Mehr/Roya reports saying no new NOTAM issued and fake-news/no-official-NOTAM disputes. Web searches run: Iran airspace closed May 24 2026 commercial flights; site:caa.gov.ir May 24 2026 Iran airspace closure; Iran NOTAM airspace closure May 24 2026; Polymarket Iran closes its airspace by May 24; site:iranintl.com/en 202605238800 Iran airspace May 23 2026; "Iran says no new NOTAM issued" "western flight suspension remains in place"; "closing the country's western airspace until May 25" "NOTAM". Leads found but not admitted: snippets/social reports of CAA NOTAM closing western airspace until May 25; sentdefender X snippet re May 22-May 25 and permission requirement; Manifold comments noting dispute and possible fake news; SafeAirspace/OPSGROUP context that Iranian western FIR remained closed or partly reopened later. Adverse lead: Roya/Mehr snippets state no new NOTAM issued, western flight suspension remains in place. Opening strategy: do not rely on outside leads as record proof; tell council the admitted record sets the rule and the dispute, while plaintiff will seek to admit official NOTAM/order and credible reporting in argument phase. Case journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md with source map, searches, adverse leads, and unresolved gaps.

Defendant opening work notes: inspected get_case/list_evidence; five case-packet text exhibits, no submitted merits evidence. Read all five packet files. Rule requires Iran-initiated major non-weather closure by 2026-05-24 23:59 ET; major means broad closure/cancellation/suspension for commercial flights across Iranian airspace or major region, or complete non-weather suspension affecting at least two listed airports (IKA, THR, MHD, SYZ, IFN). Packet expressly lacks merits proof: no official Iranian authority record, NOTAM, airport operations records, credible contemporaneous reporting packet, exact ET timing, initiation chain, or breadth analysis. Plaintiff opening acknowledges same gaps. Native investigation performed for opening: web searches for May 24/May 22-May 25 Iran NOTAM, OIIX, western Tehran FIR; official Iran AIS homepage/list checked. Current official AIS page shows current valid NOTAM list and later warnings (A1102/26, A1003/26) but not expired alleged May 22-25 item. Search leads (not admitted evidence) suggest alleged OIIX A1010/26/A0796/26, western Tehran FIR airport closures/exceptions/daylight-only permissions from May 22-May 25; sources conflict on exact times and scope. Adverse/limiting issues: alleged restriction may be airports/permissions in western FIR, not full airspace closure; exceptions may keep relevant listed airports operating; flight tracking shows behavior but not Iran initiation/scope. Strategy: opening should stress burden, source chain, scope/timing, and warn that market dispute status/regional tension/foreign advisories/airline reroutes/unadmitted snippets cannot carry proposition. Cross-turn journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md with search ledger and next evidence targets.

Arguments opportunity arguments:plaintiff. Record re-scanned with get_case/list_evidence: five starting case-packet files remain plus both openings; no defendant-submitted evidence yet. Defendant opening focuses on source chain, partial scope, exact timing, and lack of original NOTAM. Evidence plan: prove rule from ev_6c99672d9a2c_market-page, then submit source extracts establishing Iranian CAA/CAO NOTAM OIIX/A1010/26, effective through deadline, non-weather/security, broad regional scope. Searches run: "Iran closes airspace" "Trump cancels plans" "May 24"; "Iran closes airspace" "US military strikes" "May 24 2026"; "Iran closes airspace" "May 22, 2026" "May 25"; "Civil Aviation Authority" "western airspace" "May 25" Iran; "no new NOTAM" "OIIX A1010/26"; "OIIX A1010/26"; "A1010/26" "May 22" "Iran". Submitted evidence: ev_1402318e927f (Indian Express: CAA issued NOTAM OIIX A1010/26 on May 22, shutdown all airports in western Tehran FIR, valid until May 25, only eight airports incl IKA/THR/IFN/SYZ/MHD daylight/fresh CAA approval); ev_3c8bc4d23224 (Kenya Times: OIIX A1010/26 effective May 22 20:46Z to May 25 08:30Z, all airports western Tehran FIR closed except designated daylight sites, prior permissions suspended, new CAA approvals required for IFR passenger operations); ev_d8bee80dc0a7 (Abdi Media: A1010/26 closes all airports in western Tehran FIR except listed limited-operation airports; previous permissions canceled; new authorization required; valid May 22 to May 25 noon Iran time); ev_a4f6006338ea (Siasat: NOTAM OIIX A1010/26 closing airports across western Tehran FIR, limited daylight operations, fresh CAA approval). Analysis: these prove by preponderance that Iran initiated a non-weather/security closure before the deadline. Timing reaches May 24 11:59 PM ET because Kenya says valid to May 25 08:30Z and the deadline is May 25 03:59Z during US daylight time. Breadth: western Tehran FIR/western half of Iran is a major Iranian airspace region; all prior permissions suspended/fresh approvals required means commercial operations were broadly suspended with limited exceptions. The safe harbor may also be implicated by listed major airports, but plaintiff should not overclaim complete suspension for the eight named airports because they had daylight/fresh-approval operations. Weakness/adverse: no original NOTAM or official Iranian-hosted source captured; sources are secondary, some citing Gulf News. Search snippets found Roya/Mehr/social claims around June 14 saying no new NOTAM issued and western suspension remained in place, but no full reliable source was captured in time. Polymarket public page includes noisy narrative comments saying partial closure did/did not meet criteria; governance outcome is not dispositive. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant argument notes: Reviewed current case/evidence list after plaintiff argument. Plaintiff added four secondary reports: Indian Express, Kenya Times, Abdi Media, Siasat, all naming alleged NOTAM OIIX A1010/26/A1010/26 and describing May 22-May 25 restrictions in western Tehran FIR. Read all four evidence files. Strength for plaintiff: consistency on NOTAM number, issuer, non-weather/security context, May 22-May 25 period, daylight-only exceptions, fresh approval requirements. Weaknesses: no original official NOTAM text/image/PDF/official Iranian-hosted page; Indian Express attributes details to Gulf News; all are article extracts created by plaintiff; reports mix airspace language with airport closure/permit language. Material conflicts/ambiguities: Kenya says 20:46Z to 08:30Z; Abdi says 00:16 Iran time to noon Iran time. Exception airport lists conflict: Indian Express says eight airports including IKA/THR/IFN/SYZ/MHD remain operational; Kenya uses ICAO list OITR/OIKK/OIAA/OISS/OIYY/OICC/OIGG/OIAW; Abdi lists Tabriz/Kermanshah/Ahvaz/Shiraz/Yazd/Kerman/Kish/Abadan. Those conflicts matter for safe harbor and breadth. Additional official-source check performed and admitted as ev_2cc823ee050e...: official Iran AIS homepage/list/current warning pages and negative search for A1010/26 on ais.airport.ir/airport.ir. This is a provenance/custody exhibit only and does not prove the expired NOTAM never existed. Argument theory: plaintiff has shown likely some Iran aviation restriction, but not by preponderance a major closure within the exact rule because the original instrument, exact scope, and airport operations are missing and the admitted reports may prove airport/permission restrictions with exceptions rather than a broad airspace closure or complete suspension at two listed airports. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Rebuttal opportunity rebuttals:plaintiff. Record/evidence scan completed. New defendant evidence ev_2cc823ee050e read in full: official Iran AIS/AIM current-site check from 2026-06-30. It describes AIS homepage, list of valid NOTAM updated 6/29/26, current safety warning A1102/26, and A1003/26 GPS page. Searches for A1010/26 returned no results. Important: its own source description/relevance states this is a current public official-site check, not a complete historical NOTAM archive, and that it does not prove no expired May 2026 NOTAM existed. Targeted rebuttal plan: (1) concede original NOTAM custody gap but argue defendant's negative current-site search does not negate four consistent reports; (2) respond to airport/airspace distinction by focusing on western Tehran FIR as a major airspace region and commercial arriving/departing operations/permissions in that region; (3) respond to exceptions/list conflicts by noting PX-1 does not require safe harbor if major-region route is met, and limited exceptions do not defeat broad closure; (4) timing covered by PX-3 UTC period regardless of minor differences. No new evidence submitted because current record contains enough direct rebuttal material. Journal updated.

Surrebuttal notes: get_case/list_evidence checked. Plaintiff rebuttal added no new source evidence; it argues DEF-1 is only a current AIS/custody gap, not disproof, and that four secondary reports are enough under preponderance. Plaintiff's strongest points: original NOTAM not categorically required; reports converge on A1010/26; airport closures and permission restrictions in western Tehran FIR qualify as a major airspace region; safe harbor is sufficient not exclusive; timing from PX-3 covers cutoff. Defense response: accept DEF-1's limits; argue preponderance still requires proof of the rule's decisive fact, not just a probable restriction. Missing original matters because the secondary summaries themselves diverge on timing, exception airports, and whether the restriction is airspace closure, airport closures, or permission controls. Plaintiff's major-region conclusion is an inference not supported by admitted map/official text/airport operations/flight tracking. Rule excuses limited exceptions only after a broad closure is proven; here exceptions may be the operating regime. No new factual source or identifier in rebuttal warranted new source submission. Journal updated.

Closing opportunity closings:plaintiff. Record scanned via get_case/list_evidence. No new evidence after rebuttal. Closing plan: emphasize preponderance standard and PX-1's major-region route, not the safe harbor. Strongest established facts across PX-2/PX-5 and conceded as likely by defense: NOTAM OIIX/A1010/26, Iran CAA/CAO attribution, May 22-May 25, western Tehran FIR/western Iran, civil flight permits/airport operations suspended, prior permissions canceled, daylight/fresh approval exceptions. Defendant's DEF-1/PX-R1 official AIS check proves only a current-source custody gap; it expressly says it is not a complete historical NOTAM archive and does not prove an expired May 2026 NOTAM never existed. Strongest defense point is no original NOTAM and variation in exception-airport lists/times. Response: preponderance; variations are peripheral; PX-3 UTC timing covers the deadline; all reports agree on broad regional permission/operation suspension; PX-1 says limited exceptions do not disqualify broad closure. No new evidence or technical reports permitted in closing. Journal updated.

Closing notes: final get_case/list_evidence scan performed at defense closing. Plaintiff closing adds no new evidence. Record is four plaintiff secondary reports, defense official AIS current-source/custody check, and case packet. Strongest plaintiff point: reports converge enough to make some Iran CAA late-May restriction likely and PX-3 timing covers cutoff if accurate. Defense closing should not overstate; focus on whether plaintiff proved the specific market rule: major closure of airspace. Missing original NOTAM prevents deciding legal scope; reports describe airport closures/permissions and exceptions, not reproduced airspace closure text. No admitted map, official text, airport operations, or flight tracking establishes western FIR sector as a major region or broad suspension of commercial flights transiting/arriving/departing. Exception lists conflict and may define the operative regime, not limited exceptions to a proven broad closure. Safe harbor not proven because reports identify listed airports as operating in some form. Final ask: not proven by preponderance, while acknowledging likely restriction existed. Journal updated.
